name: Tennessee
examName: Tennessee Real Estate Affiliate Broker Examination

examFacts.notes: Administered by PSI for the Tennessee Real Estate Commission (TREC), part of the Tennessee Department of Commerce and Insurance.

intro:
The Tennessee real estate affiliate broker exam trips up plenty of first-time candidates. The Tennessee Real Estate Commission (TREC) gives it through PSI as two separate portions, an 80-question national section and a 40-question Tennessee state section, for 120 scored questions and 240 minutes of testing time. You need 70 percent on each portion and the two are scored separately, so a strong national score won't rescue a weak state score. That gap on the state section is why Tennessee-specific preparation matters more than another round of national review.

State-specific prep matters more here than almost anywhere else. The Tennessee state portion leans on the Tennessee Real Estate Broker Act of 1973, the designated agent and facilitator brokerage relationships that Tennessee defines by statute, the Tennessee residential property condition disclosure, the Real Estate Education and Recovery Account, the errors and omissions insurance every licensee must carry, and the trust and escrow rules TREC enforces. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need Tennessee-specific practice questions.

Below are 20 free Tennessee real estate test questions and answers from RealReady's full Tennessee bank. Each tests a specific topic with the correct answer and a plain-English explanation under the card. Read each one, commit to an answer, then reveal. Treat it like a real TREC practice test, and the ones you miss show you exactly what to focus on next.

Get the RealReady app on iPhone, iPad, or Android for the rest of the Tennessee question bank. The full app includes short articles that walk you through the why behind each topic, detailed explanations on every question, a missed-question drill mode, and progress tracking that shows your per-category accuracy.

faq.q: How many questions are on the Tennessee real estate exam?
faq.a: The Tennessee affiliate broker exam has 120 scored questions, split into an 80-question national portion and a 40-question Tennessee state portion. PSI also mixes in 5 to 10 unscored experimental questions that don't count toward your result. You get 240 minutes in total, 160 for the national portion and 80 for the state portion.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the Tennessee real estate exam?
faq.a: You need 70 percent on each portion, and the two are scored separately. That works out to 56 of 80 correct on the national portion and 28 of 40 on the state portion. You have to pass both to get licensed, so a high score on one portion doesn't make up for falling short on the other.

faq.q: How much does the Tennessee real estate exam cost?
faq.a: PSI charges $63 to sit both portions of the affiliate broker exam. If you only need to retake a single portion, it's $37 for the national portion or $26 for the state portion. After you pass, Tennessee's initial affiliate broker license fee is $91, and affiliate broker is the entry-level license the state issues.

faq.q: How long is the Tennessee real estate exam?
faq.a: 240 minutes in total. PSI gives you 160 minutes for the 80-question national portion and 80 minutes for the 40-question state portion, plus up to 15 minutes for an introductory tutorial that doesn't count against your testing time. Each portion is timed and scored on its own, and your result appears on screen as soon as you finish.

faq.q: Who administers the Tennessee real estate exam?
faq.a: The Tennessee Real Estate Affiliate Broker Examination is administered by PSI for the Tennessee Real Estate Commission (TREC). You register and schedule your exam date through PSI.

faq.q: Is the Tennessee real estate exam hard?
faq.a: Plenty of first-time candidates pass, but the exam earns its reputation, and the state portion is where people stumble. The national portion is manageable for most candidates who actually studied. The state portion catches people off guard, because Tennessee's brokerage-relationship rules, disclosure requirements, and the way the Real Estate Commission is structured don't match what national prep material teaches. Honest practice on Tennessee-specific questions is usually the difference between passing the first time and paying to retake a portion.

faq.q: What's on the Tennessee real estate exam?
faq.a: The 80-question national portion covers general real estate principles like agency, contracts, financing, property ownership, valuation, and fair housing. The 40-question Tennessee state portion focuses on the duties and powers of the Real Estate Commission, Tennessee licensing and advertising rules, broker and affiliate relationships, the handling of documents and trust and escrow funds, agency and disclosure issues including designated agents and facilitators, and special areas like timeshare and property management. The state-specific material carries more weight than its share of questions suggests, since it's the part most candidates have never seen before.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the Tennessee real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish your 60 hours of pre-license coursework, the highest-leverage move is grinding through Tennessee-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. National practice tests help with the 80 national questions but won't catch you on Tennessee's brokerage and disclosure rules. The RealReady app gives you the full Tennessee question bank, plus a missed-question mode and per-category progress tracking so you can drill your weak spots. Most users study 30 to 60 minutes a day for two to four weeks before sitting the exam.

The 20 Tennessee practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the Tennessee bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the Tennessee real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated Tennessee question bank built for the Tennessee real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the Tennessee law that trips people up, like the Tennessee Real Estate Broker Act of 1973, the designated agent and facilitator relationships, and the Real Estate Education and Recovery Account.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward Tennessee's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
